notes: Full Playthrough. All Nazis are dead now.
title: Wolfenstein II: The New Colossus

cpu: AMD Ryzen 3 2200G with Radeon Vega Graphics
title: Wolfenstein II: The New Colossus

notes: About midway (in Area 51) I get the dreaded "str_online_connection_error_03". Before that happened, the game worked flawlessly
title: Wolfenstein II: The New Colossus

notes: Played the game through twice with several different version of proton and found no issues or crashes.  Frame rate seemed smooth and stable.
title: Wolfenstein II: The New Colossus

notes: Added +r_renderAPI 1 as launch option prior to first launch - probably not necessary at all. Several hours in the game I experienced a crash once, which I could not reproduce. Given my modest hardware the game runs quite fluidly on 2560x1440 on the high quality preset.
title: Wolfenstein II: The New Colossus

notes: Nothing to improve here, almost unnoticeable because it runs on vulkan natively
title: Wolfenstein II: The New Colossus

notes: In the cutscenes, the mouths are rendered incorrectly. But the game can be played without error.
title: Wolfenstein II: The New Colossus

notes: My entire system freezes on starting the game. Not even holding down the power button works, to shut it down.
title: Wolfenstein II: The New Colossus

notes: used to work perfectly, then my mesa got updated to 19.1.1 and it won't start, giving "vkCreateDevice failed with error (VK_ERROR_FEATURE_NOT_PRESENT)". I downgraded the vulkan driver to 19.0.6 and it worked perfectly again. however i also got it  working with mesa 19.1.1 installed by running the amdvlk driver, unfortunately the water surface is not rendered then
title: Wolfenstein II: The New Colossus

notes: Almost refunded it, because I couldn't get past menu(I saw the menu but it freezes, and only hard reset will help). Then after few hours of debugging and googling I found that adding +r_disableSteamOverlay 1 +r_enableAsyncCompute 0 to launch options fixes the problem. Also added +r_renderAPI 1 as recommended by other reports. Game runs perfectly with the highest settings 1440p.
title: Wolfenstein II: The New Colossus

notes: mesa 19.1.2 fixed the VK_ERROR_INITIALIZATION_FAILED error, runs perfectly, however the water surface is still not rendered unfortunately. i am certain it was working for me at some point, might also be a configuration change on my end though
title: Wolfenstein II: The New Colossus

notes: No tweaks except adding "+r_renderAPI 1" to the launch options. Running ultra settings, there is a warning about running out of dedicated video memory but i just dismiss the warning and keep playing. The game is super smooth at most times except from some parts with a lot of detail (especially outdoors) where the fps goes down a bit, still rather playable though. My rig is getting a bit outdated, so i surely can't complain :)
title: Wolfenstein II: The New Colossus

notes: Excellent performance and worked straight off, although I did disable steam overlay as other suggested. I've not tried it on Windows but I can't see it running any better. It runs 4k 60fps at highest graphical setting with locked V-Sync, which is definitely smoother for me than  adaptive v-sync. I just reduced the anti-aliasing down to a mid-range value.
title: Wolfenstein II: The New Colossus

notes: Works perfect after diable ASYNC Engine. If not, game crash.
title: Wolfenstein II: The New Colossus
